summary: This PR adds a minimal in-memory token-bucket rate limiter as an example (`examples/token_bucket.py`). The `TokenBucket` class tracks per-key token counts in a plain dict, using lazy refill — tokens are replenished on demand at check time rather than via a background timer — which keeps the implementation simple and dependency-free. The `allow(key, cost)` method returns a boolean and is the single entry point for callers. The design is intentionally scoped to single-process use with no eviction, making it suitable as a lightweight illustrative example rather than a production-grade distributed limiter.
